title: agelanthus subulatus
intro: Agelanthus subulatus, also known as the "witchweed," is a parasitic flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family that grows on the roots of other plants, such as Acacia species. The medicinal properties of Agelanthus subulatus include its ability to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of these conditions. Agelanthus subulatus is traditionally prepared as a tea or decoction for its medicinal uses, and it is also used in some herbal preparations to treat various health issues.
